pymetis
  • API Reference
  • engine.inputs
  • View page source

engine.inputs

Base classes for pipeline input frame handling.

class pymetis.engine.inputs.input.PipelineInput[source]

Bases: object

This class encapsulates a single logical input to a recipe: - either a single file, or a line in the SOF (see SinglePipelineInput) - or a set of equivalent files (see MultiplePipelineInput)

It is a relatively thin wrapper over the inner Item.

load_frameset(frameset)[source]

Load the associated frames.

Fills the internal buffer with the contents of the frames (not the actual data yet).

Return type:

None

Parameters:

frameset (cpl.ui.FrameSet)

abstractmethod set_cpl_attributes()[source]

Set CPL attributes of loaded frames. ToDO: is this really necessary?

classmethod required()[source]

Returns whether this pipeline input is required. Used during validation.

Return type:

bool

classmethod multiplicity()[source]

Returns the multiplicity of the input (‘1’ or ‘N’)

Return type:

str

__init__(frameset)[source]

Verify that all required class attributes are defined and promote to the most specialized derived class depending on the input frameset.

Parameters:

frameset (cpl.ui.FrameSet)

Item: ClassVar[type[DataItem]] = None
abstractmethod validate()[source]

Verify that the input has all the required frames and that they are valid themselves. There is no default logic, implementation is fully deferred to derived classes.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod load_structure()[source]

Load the input structure and store as data items, but without data yet.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod load_data(extension=None)[source]

Load the actual data content and return it.

Return type:

Union[ImageList, Image, Table]

Parameters:

extension (int | str)

print_debug(*, offset=0)[source]

Print a short description of the tags, optionally with a small offset (N spaces).

Return type:

None

Parameters:

offset (int)

as_dict()[source]
Return type:

dict[str, Any]

final classmethod extended_description_line(name=None)[source]

Produce ae extended description line for man page.

Return type:

str

Parameters:

name (str)

abstract property contents

Return the data contents (either the item or items)

abstractmethod valid_frames()[source]

Return a FrameSet containing all valid, used frames. This is abstract as it differs significantly for Single and Multiple Inputs.

Return type:

FrameSet

abstractmethod used_frames()[source]

Return a FrameSet containing all used frames.

Return type:

FrameSet

abstractmethod use()[source]

Mark the input as used

Return type:

None

class pymetis.engine.inputs.single.SinglePipelineInput[source]

Bases: PipelineInput

A pipeline input that expects exactly a single frame to be present.

__init__(frameset)[source]

Verify that all required class attributes are defined and promote to the most specialized derived class depending on the input frameset.

Parameters:

frameset (cpl.ui.FrameSet)

load_structure()[source]

Load the input structure and store as data items, but without data yet.

Return type:

None

load_data(extension=None)[source]

Load the actual data content and return it.

Return type:

Union[Image, Table]

Parameters:

extension (str)

set_cpl_attributes()[source]

Set CPL attributes of loaded frames. ToDO: is this really necessary?

validate()[source]

Run all the required instantiation time checks. Currently, we only check whether there is exactly one frame (if required) or at most one (if not required).

as_dict()[source]
Return type:

dict[str, Any]

property contents

Return the data contents (either the item or items)

use()[source]

Mark the input as used

Return type:

Self

valid_frames()[source]

Return a FrameSet containing all valid, used frames. This is abstract as it differs significantly for Single and Multiple Inputs.

Return type:

FrameSet

used_frames()[source]

Return a FrameSet containing all used frames.

Return type:

FrameSet

class pymetis.engine.inputs.multiple.MultiplePipelineInput[source]

Bases: PipelineInput

A pipeline input that expects multiple similar frames, such as a raw processor.

__init__(frameset)[source]

Verify that all required class attributes are defined and promote to the most specialized derived class depending on the input frameset.

Parameters:

frameset (cpl.ui.FrameSet)

load_structure()[source]

Load the items inside this PipelineInput.

Return type:

None

load_data(extension=None)[source]

Load a list of data items from a FrameSet, all corresponding to the same extension HDU. The items should be a homogeneous set of CPL Images. # ToDO make sure that there can never be multiple tables, only images

Parameters:

extension (int | str) –

The extension of the items to load, can be

  • int, in which case the extension will be fetched by number;

  • str, in which case EXTNAME will be matched.

Returns:

A CPL ImageLisŧ containing the loaded images.

Return type:

cpl.core.ImageList

set_cpl_attributes()[source]

Set the required CPL attributes from the associated DataItem.

validate()[source]

Verify that the input has all the required frames and that they are valid themselves. There is no default logic, implementation is fully deferred to derived classes.

as_dict()[source]

Return a dictionary representation of the input.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

property contents

Return the data contents (either the item or items)

use()[source]

Mark the input as used

Return type:

Self

valid_frames()[source]

Return a list of valid frames.

# FixMe: currently returns everything, but should only return valid frames. :rtype: FrameSet :return:

cpl.ui.FrameSet : a list of valid frames

Return type:

cpl.ui.FrameSet

used_frames()[source]

Return a FrameSet containing all used frames.

Return type:

FrameSet

class pymetis.engine.inputs.inputset.PipelineInputSet[source]

Bases: ABC

The PipelineInputSet class is a utility class for a recipe dealing with the input data. It reads and filters the input FrameSet, categorizes the frames by their metadata, and finally stores them in its own attributes for further use. It also provides verification mechanisms and methods for extraction of additional information from the frames.

Every RecipeImpl should have exactly one InputSet class (possibly but not necessarily shared by multiple recipes). Currently, we define them as internal classes of the corresponding RecipeImpl, but in Python it does not really matter much and does not imply any particular relationship between the classes – it is just a namespacing convention.

__init__(frameset)[source]

Filter the input frameset, capture frames that match criteria and assign them to your own attributes. By default, there is nothing: no inputs, no tag_parameters. This feels hacky but makes it much more comfortable as you do not need to define Inputs manually.

Parameters:

frameset (cpl.ui.FrameSet)

classmethod specialize(**parameters)[source]

Specialize all input classes within this input set, based on tunable parameters.

Return type:

None

classmethod list_classes()[source]

List all input classes within this input set.

Warning: uses introspection, feels hacky. # ToDo Maybe make this more explicit someday?

Return type:

list[tuple[str, type[PipelineInput]]]

classmethod list_descriptions()[source]
Return type:

str

validate()[source]
Return type:

None

Validate the inputset:
  • see that all inputs are loaded

    • and that they are themselves valid

    • and that they are processing compatible data (same detector, etc.)

  • parse the tag parameters

    • and assign their values as attributes of the inputset

print_debug(*, offset=0)[source]
Return type:

None

Parameters:

offset (int)

as_dict()[source]

Return a dict representation of the input patterns.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

property valid_frames: cpl.ui.FrameSet
property used_frames: cpl.ui.FrameSet

Return the frames that actually affect the output anyhow (if a frame is not listed here, the output without that frame should be identical

  • [HB]: also includes frames that do not contribute any pixel data,

    for instance, discarded outliers (without them a different frame might be an outlier)

# FixMe: Currently this only ensures that frames are loaded, not actually used! # FixMe: This is not a trivial problem though, maybe it will have to be marked manually everytime.
